heading: Requirements of certifying agents
label: 6515 Requirements of certifying agents

children.heading: Ability to implement requirements
children.label: a Ability to implement requirements
children.text: To be accredited as a certifying agent under , a governing State official or a person shall be able to fully implement the applicable organic certification program established under this chapter.section 6514 of this title

children.heading: Inspectors
children.label: b Inspectors
children.text: Any certifying agent shall employ a sufficient number of inspectors to implement the applicable organic certification program established under this chapter, as determined by the Secretary.
agree to carry out the provisions of this chapter; and
agree to such other terms and conditions as the Secretary determines appropriate.
agree to hold the Secretary harmless for any failure on the part of the certifying agent to carry out the provisions of this chapter; and

furnish reasonable security, in an amount determined by the Secretary, for the purpose of protecting the rights of participants in the applicable organic certification program established under this chapter.

children.heading: Compliance with program
children.label: e Compliance with program
children.text: Any certifying agent shall fully comply with the terms and conditions of the applicable organic certification program implemented under this chapter.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: f Confidentiality
children.text: Except as provided in , any certifying agent shall maintain strict confidentiality with respect to its clients under the applicable organic certification program and may not disclose to third parties (with the exception of the Secretary or the applicable governing State official) any business related information concerning such client obtained while implementing this chapter.section 6506(a)(9) of this title

carry out any inspections of any operation in which such certifying agent, or employee of such certifying agent has, or has had, a commercial interest, including the provision of consultancy services;
accept payment, gifts, or favors of any kind from the business inspected other than prescribed fees; or
provide advice concerning organic practices or techniques for a fee, other than fees established under such program.

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: h Administrator
children.text: A certifying agent that is a private person shall nominate the individual who controls the day-to-day operation of the agent.

children.heading: Noncompliance
children.label: 1 Noncompliance
children.text: If the Secretary or the governing State official (if applicable) determines that a certifying agent or an entity acting as an agent of the certifying agent is not properly adhering to the provisions of this chapter, the Secretary or such governing State official may suspend such certifying agent’s accreditation.

children.heading: Oversight of certifying offices and foreign operations
children.label: 2 Oversight of certifying offices and foreign operations
i
children.text: an office operating in a foreign country; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an office operating in the United States, including an office acting on behalf of a foreign-domiciled entity.

children.heading: Process for resuming operations following suspension
children.label: B Process for resuming operations following suspension
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for a process that is otherwise consistent with this section that authorizes a suspended office to resume operations.

children.heading: Effect on certified operations
children.label: 3 Effect on certified operations
children.text: If the accreditation of a certifying agent is suspended under paragraph (1), the Secretary or the governing State official (if applicable) shall promptly determine whether farming or handling operations certified by such certifying agent may retain their organic certification.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: j Notice
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which a new certifying office performing certification activities opens, an accredited certifying agent shall notify the Secretary of the opening.
